==================================================
ASSESSMENT DATE: 2026-03-07
==================================================

ASSESSMENT SUMMARY:
Mrs. Smith is a woman with severe irritable bowel syndrome causing frequent diarrhea and chronic pain that has rendered her unable to work or manage household activities for two years. She experiences significant depression with social withdrawal, sleeping excessively, and loss of motivation. Her husband is overwhelmed managing her care plus a special needs child while working full-time, creating a high-stress family environment. The IBS symptoms interrupt all daily activities, requiring 30-40 minutes in the bathroom multiple times daily, preventing completion of basic tasks like cooking and cleaning.

ADL STATUS: Moderate ADL impairment primarily due to IBS symptoms and depression affecting energy and motivation

ADL Score: 6

IADL STATUS: Significant IADL impairment affecting household management and community activities

IADL Score: 5

SAFETY: Primary safety concern is depression and social isolation, low physical safety risks

MENTAL HEALTH: Significant depression with sleep disturbance, social withdrawal, and feelings of desperation

CAREGIVER:
Primary caregiver: Husband (Davis)
Caregiver stress: High stress and burnout - feels like he has no helper, everything is on him, frustrated and angry
Care gaps: Significant gaps when husband is at work and managing special needs child
mobility_status: Limited mobility due to frequent bathroom needs and fatigue
cognitive_status: Alert and oriented but experiencing depression
living_situation: With spouse and special needs child
care_plan:
SHORT-TERM GOALS (30 days):
• Establish daily routine with caregiver support
• Improve nutritional intake with IBS-appropriate meals
• Reduce social isolation through companion care
• Provide respite for overwhelmed spouse

LONG-TERM GOALS (90+ days):
• Improve depression symptoms through consistent support
• Develop coping strategies for IBS management
• Strengthen family relationships through reduced caregiver stress
• Explore potential return to some productive activities

MAINTENANCE GOALS:
• Maintain stable mood and prevent further depression
• Ensure adequate nutrition despite IBS limitations
• Prevent caregiver burnout through ongoing respite
special_requirements:
• IBS-friendly meal preparation: Meals appropriate for irritable bowel syndrome, avoiding trigger foods [Dietary]
• Flexible scheduling around bathroom needs: Care schedule must accommodate frequent bathroom breaks lasting 30-40 minutes [Scheduling]
• Depression-informed care approach: Caregivers trained in supporting clients with depression and motivation issues [Mental Health]
preferred_times: Flexible due to unpredictable bathroom needs
